author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXLI

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 1. …The Psalm which we have just sung is in many parts somewhat obscure.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When by the help of the Lord what has been said shall begin to be expounded and explained, ye will see that ye are hearing things which ye knew already.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But for this cause are they said in manifold ways, that variety of expression may remove all weariness of the truth.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Lord, I have cried unto Thee, hear Thou me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This we all can say.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This not I alone say: whole Christ saith it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is said rather in the name of the Body: for He too, when He was here and bore our flesh, prayed; and when He prayed, drops of blood streamed down from His whole Body.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So is it written in the Gospel: “Jesus prayed earnestly, and His sweat was as it were great drops of blood.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is this flowing of sweat from His whole Body, but the suffering of martyrs from the whole Church?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Listen unto the voice of my prayer, while I cry unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou thoughtest the business of crying already finished, when thou saidst, “I have cried unto Thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou hast cried; yet think not thyself safe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If tribulation be finished, crying is finished: but if tribulation remain for the Church, for the Body of Christ, even to the end of the world, let it not only say, “I have cried unto Thee,” but also, “Listen unto the voice of my prayer.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Let my prayer be set forth in Thy sight as incense, and the lifting up of my hands an evening sacrifice” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That this is wont to be understood of the Head Himself, every Christian acknowledgeth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For when the day was now sinking towards evening, the Lord upon the Cross “laid down His life to take it again,” did not lose it against His will.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Still we too are figured there.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what of Him hung upon the tree, save what He took of us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how can it be that the Father should leave and abandon His only begotten Son, especially when He is one God with Him?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet, fixing our weakness upon the Cross, where, as the Apostle saith, “our old man is crucified with Him,” He cried out in the voice of that our “old man,” “Why hast Thou forsaken Me?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That then is the “evening sacrifice,” the Passion of the Lord, the Cross of the Lord, the offering of a salutary Victim, the whole burnt offering acceptable to God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That “evening sacrifice” produced, in His Resurrection, a morning offering.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Prayer then, purely directed from a faithful heart, riseth like incense from a hallowed altar.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nought is more delightful than the odour of the Lord: such odour let all have who believe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 4. …“Set, O Lord, a watch before my mouth, and a door of restraint around my lips” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He said not a barrier of restraint, but “a door of restraint.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: A door is opened as well as shut.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If then it be a “door,” let it be both opened and shut; opened, to confession of sin; closed, to excusing sin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So will it be a “door of restraint,” not of ruin.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For what doth this “door of restraint” profit us?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What doth Christ pray in the name of His Body?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “That Thou turn not aside My heart to wicked words” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “My heart”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The heart of My Church; the heart, that is, of My Body.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But when thine heart hath not been turned aside, O member of Christ, when thy heart hath not been turned aside “to wicked words, to making excuses in sins, with men that work in iniquity,” thou shalt also not unite with their elect.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For this followeth, “And I will not unite with their elect.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are “their elect”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those who justify themselves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are their elect?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Those “who trust in themselves that they are righteous, and despise others,” as the Pharisee said in the temple, “Lord, I thank Thee that I am not as other men are.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who are their elect?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This Man, if He were a prophet, would know what manner of woman this is that touched His feet.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Here thou recognisest the words of that other Pharisee, who invited our Lord to his house; when the woman of that city, who was a sinner, came and approached His Feet.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For even this woman herself, “if her heart had turned aside to wicked words,” would not have lacked wherewith to defend her sins.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do not women daily, her equals in defilement, but not her equals in confession, harlots, adulteresses, doers of shameful deeds, defend their sins?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If they have not been seen, they deny them: if they have been caught and convicted, or have done their deeds openly, they defend them.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And how easy is their defence, how ready, yet how headlong; how common, yet how blasphemous!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Had God not willed it, I had not done it: God willed it: fortune willed it: fate willed it.”…These are the defences of “the elect” of this world.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But let the members of Christ, the Body of Christ, say, let Christ say in the name of His Body, “Turn not Thou aside, My Heart, to wicked words,” etc., “and I will not unite with their elect.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “With men that work wickedness.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What wickedness?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me mention some sinful wickedness of theirs.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let me tell you one open sinful wickedness, which they acknowledge.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They say, it is better for a man to be an usurer than a husbandman.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askest the reason, and they assign one.…He vexeth the members of Christ, who cleanseth the earth with a furrow: he vexeth the members of Christ, who pulleth grass from the earth: he vexeth the members of Christ, who plucketh an apple from a tree.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To avoid committing their imaginary murders in the farm, he committeth real murders in usury.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He dealeth no bread to the needy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See whether there can be greater unrighteousness than this righteousness.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He dealeth not bread to the hungry.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou askest, wherefore?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Lest the beggar receive the life which is in the bread, which they call a member of God, the substance of God, and bind it in flesh.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then do ye? why do ye eat?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have ye not flesh?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yes; but we, they say, forasmuch as we are enlightened by faith in Manes, by our prayers and our Psalms, forasmuch as we are elect, we cleanse thereby that bread, and transmit it into the treasure-house of the heavens.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such are the elect, that they are not to be saved by God, but saviours of God.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And this is Christ, they say, crucified in the whole universe.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I received in the Gospel Christ a Saviour, but ye are in your books the saviours of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Plainly ye are blasphemers of Christ, and therefore not to be saved by Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore lest a crumb be given to the hungry, and in the crumb a member of Christ suffer, is the hungry to die of hunger?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: False mercy to a crumb causeth true murder of a man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But who are their elect?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Turn not thou aside, my heart, to wicked words, and I will not unite with their elect.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The righteous One shall amend me in mercy, and convict me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold the sinner confessing.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He desireth to be amended in mercy, rather than praised deceitfully.…“Shall convict me,” but “in mercy:” shall convict, yet hateth not: yea, shall all the more convict, because He hateth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And why doth he therefore give thanks?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because, “rebuke a wise man, and he will love thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The righteous One shall amend me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Because He persecuteth thee?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He requireth rather amending himself, who amendeth in hate.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wherefore then doth He amend?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And shall convict me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the oil of a sinner shall not enrich my head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My head shall not grow by flattery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Undue praise is flattery: undue praise of a flatterer is “the oil of a sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore men too, when they have mocked any one with false praise, say, “I have anointed his head.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Love then to be “convicted by the righteous One in mercy;” love not to be praised by a sinner in mockery.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Have oil in yourselves, and ye shall not seek the “oil of a sinner.”…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou sayest to me, What am I doing?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I am beset with flatterers; they cease not to besiege me; they praise in me what I would not, that praise in me what I hold in little esteem; what I hold dear they blame in me; flatterers, treacherous, deceivers.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For instance, “Gaiuseius is a great man, great, learned, wise; but why is he a Christian?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For great is his learning, great his reading, great his wisdom.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If great is his wisdom, approve of his being a Christian; if great his learning, learnedly hath he chosen.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In fine, what thou revilest, that pleaseth him whom thou praisest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That praise sweeteneth not: it is “the oil of a sinner.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Yet ceaseth he not to speak so.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him not therewith “fatten thy head;” that is, rejoice not in such things; agree not to such things; consent not to such things; rejoice not in such things; and then, if he have applied to thee the oil of flattery, yet hath thy head remained as it was, it has not been puffed up, it hath not swollen.…“For still shall My word be well-pleasing to them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wait awhile: now they revile Me, saith Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the early times of the Christians, the Christians were blamed on all sides.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Wait as yet; and “My word shall be well-pleasing to them.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The time shall come when they shall conquer thousands of men, who shall beat their breasts, and say, “Forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even now, how many remain who blush to beat their breasts?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them then blame us: let us bear it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let them blame; let them hate, accuse, detract; “still shall My word be well-pleasing to them;” the time shall come when My word shall please them.…O wordy defence of iniquity!

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Verily now whole nations say this, and the thunder of nations beating their breasts ceaseth not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly do the clouds thunder, wherein now God dwelleth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Where is now that wordiness, where that boasting, “I am righteous; nought of ill have I done”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Verily, when thou hast contemplated in Holy Scripture the law of righteousness, how far soever thou hast advanced, thou shalt find thyself a sinner.…What sort of man am I now speaking of, brethren?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I speak of him who worshippeth God alone, who confesseth Christ, who knoweth the Father and the Son and the Holy Ghost to be one God; who committeth not fornication against Him; who worshippeth not devils; who seeketh him not aid from the devil; who holdeth the Catholic Church; whom no one complaineth of as cheating; under whose oppression no weak neighbour groaneth; who assaileth not another’s wife; who is content with his own, or even without his own, in such wise as is lawful, and as Apostolical discipline permitteth, with consent of both, or when she is not yet married.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Even he who is such as this, is yet overtaken in such things as I have mentioned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For all these daily sins then what is our hope, save to say with humble heart in the Lord’s Prayer, while we defend not our sins, but confess them, “Forgive us our debts, as we forgive our debtors;” and to “have an Advocate with the Father, Jesus Christ the righteous,” that He may be “the propitiation for our sins”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: See what followeth: “their judges have been swallowed up beside the Rock” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is, “swallowed up beside the Rock?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: That Rock was Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have been swallowed up beside the Rock.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Beside,” that is, compared, as judges, as mighty, powerful, learned: they are called “their judges,” as judging about morals, and laying down their opinions.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Aristotle said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Set him beside the Rock, and he is swallowed up.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who is Aristotle? let him hear, “Christ hath said,” and he trembleth among the dead.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: This Pythagoras said, that Plato said.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Set them beside the Rock, compare their authority to the authority of the Gospel, compare the proud to the Crucified.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Say we to them “Ye have written your words in the hearts of the proud; He hath planted His Cross in the hearts of kings: finally, He died, and rose again; ye are dead, and I will not ask how ye rise again.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So “their judges have been swallowed up beside” that “Rock.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: So long do their words seem somewhat, till they are compared with the Rock.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Therefore if any of them be found to have said what Christ too hath said, we congratulate him, but we follow him not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But he came before Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If any man speak what is true, is he therefore before the Truth itself?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Regard Christ, O man, not when He came to thee, but when He made thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The sick man too might say, “But I took to my bed before the physician came to me.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Why, for that very reason has He come last, because thou first has sickened.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall hear My Words, for they have prevailed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: My Words have prevailed over their words.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They have spoken clever things, I true things.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To praise one who talketh well is one thing, to praise One who speaketh truth is another.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “They shall hear My Words, for they have prevailed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How have they prevailed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who of them has been taken offering sacrifice, when such things were forbidden by the law, and has not denied it?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Who of them has been taken worshipping an idol, and has not exclaimed, “I did it not,” and feared lest he should be convicted?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such servants hath the devil.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But how have the Words of the Lord prevailed?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Behold, I send you forth as sheep in the midst of wolves.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not those who kill the body,” etc. He gave them fear, He suggested hope, He kindled love.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Fear not death,” He saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Do ye fear death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I die first.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear ye, lest a hair of your head perish?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I first rise again in the flesh uninjured.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Rightly have ye heard His Words, for they have prevailed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They spake, and were slain; they fell, and yet stood.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what was the result of so many deaths of martyrs, save that those words prevailed, and the earth being, so to speak, watered by the blood of Christ’s witnesses, the cross of the Church shot up everywhere?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How have they “prevailed”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We have said already, when they were preached by men who feared not.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Feared not what?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Neither banishment, nor losses, nor death, nor crucifixion: for it was not death alone that they did not fear; but even crucifixion, a death than which none was thought more accursed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It the Lord endured, that His disciples might not only not fear death, but not even that kind of death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then these things are said by men that fear not, they have prevailed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What then have all those deaths of the martyrs accomplished?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Listen: “As the fatness of the earth is spread over the earth, our bones have been scattered beside the pit” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The bones” of the martyrs, that is, the bodies of the witnesses of Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The martyrs were slain, and they who slew them seemed to prevail.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They prevailed by persecution, that the words of Christ might prevail by preaching.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And what was the result of the deaths of the saints?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What meaneth, “the fatness of the earth is spread over the earth”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We know that everything that is refuse is the fatness of the earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The things which are, as it were, contemptible to men, enrich the earth.…“Precious in the sight of the Lord is the death of His saints.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: As it is contemptible to the world, so is it precious to the husbandman.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he knoweth the use thereof, and its rich juice; he knoweth what he desireth, what he seeketh, whence the fertile crop ariseth; but this world despiseth it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Know ye not that “God hath chosen the contemptible things of the world, and those which are not, like as those which are, that the things which are may be brought to nought”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: From the dunghill was Peter lifted up, and Paul; when they were put to death, they were despised: now, the earth having been enriched by them, and the cross of the Church springing up, behold, all that is noble and chief in the world, even the emperor himself, cometh to Rome, and whither does he hasten? to the temple of the emperor, or the memorial of the fisherman?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For unto Thee, Lord, are mine eyes; in Thee have I hoped, take not Thou away my life” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they were tortured in persecutions, and many failed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It occurreth to him that many have failed, many have been in hazard, and as it were in the midst of the tribulation of persecution is sent forth the voice of one praying; “For unto Thee, Lord, are mine eyes:” I care not what they threaten who stand around, “unto Thee, Lord, are mine eyes.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: More do I fix mine eye on Thy promises than on their threats.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I know what Thou hast suffered for me, what Thou hast promised me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Keep me from the trap which they have laid for me” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What was the trap?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “If thou consentest, I spare thee.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: In the trap was set the bait of the present life; if the bird love this bait, it falleth into the trap: but if the bird be able to say, “The day of man have I not desired: Thou knowest:” “He shall pluck his feet out of the net,” etc. Two things he hath mentioned to be distinguished the one from the other: the trap he said was set by persecutors; the stumbling-blocks came from those who have consented and apostatised: and from both he desires to be guarded.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On the one side they threaten and rage, on the other consent and fall: I fear lest the one be such, that I fear him; the other such, that I imitate him.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “This I do to thee, if thou consent not.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Keep me from the trap,” etc. “Behold, thy brother hath already consented.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “And from the stumbling-blocks,” etc.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Sinners shall fall into his nets” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not all sinners, certain sinners, who are so great sinners, as to love this life to such a degree as to prefer it to everlasting life, “shall fall into his trap.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what sayest thou?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Shall they that are such, thinkest thou, fall into his nets? what of Thy disciples, O Christ?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Behold, when persecution was raging, when they all “left Thee alone, and went every one to his own:” lo! they who were closest to Thee, in Thy trial and persecution, when Thine enemies demanded Thee to be crucified, abandoned Thee.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And that bold one, who had promised Thee that he would go with Thee even unto death, heard from the Physician what was being done in him, the sick man.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For being in a fever, he had said he was whole; but the Lord touched the vein of his heart.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Then came the trial; then came the test; then came the accusation; and now, questioned not by some great power, but by a humble slave, and that a woman, questioned by a handmaid, he yielded; he denied thrice.…“He wept bitterly,” it saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet was he fitted to suffer.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: To him was said, “Thou shalt follow Me afterwards.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Hereafter he was to be firm, having been strengthened by the Lord’s Resurrection.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Not yet then was it time that those “bones” should be “scattered beside the pit.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For see how many failed, even to those who first hung on His mouth; even they failed.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am alone, until I pass over:” for this followeth in the Psalm.…

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Pascha, as they say who know, and who have explained to us what to read, meaneth “Passover.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: When then the Lord’s Passion was about to come, the Evangelist, as though he would use this very word, saith, “When the hour was come that Jesus should pass over to the Father.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: We hear then of Pascha in this verse, “I am alone, until I pass over.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: After Pascha I shall no longer be alone, after passing-over I shall no longer be alone.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Many shall imitate Me, many shall follow Me.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: And if afterward they shall follow, what shall be the case now?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “I am alone, until I pass over.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that the Lord saith in this Psalm, “I am alone, until I pass over”?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is it that we have expounded?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If we have understood it, listen to His own words in the Gospel.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Except a corn of wheat fall into the ground and die, it abideth alone; but if it die, it beareth much fruit.”…Therefore He was alone before He was put to death.…So far was any from dying for the Name, that is, for confessing the Name of Christ, before that Corn of wheat fell into the ground, that even John, who was slain just before Him, being given by a wicked king to a dancing woman, was not put to death because he confessed Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Of course he might have been put to death for this, and that by many.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: If for another reason he was put to death by one man, how much more might he have been put to death by those very men, who put Christ to death?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For John gave testimony to Christ.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: They who heard Christ, wished to slay Him; the man who gave testimony to Him they slew not.…He is not slain by the Jews who gave free testimony to Christ, whom the Jews slew; he is slain by Herod, because he said to him, “It is not lawful for thee to have thy brother’s wife.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For his brother had not died without issue.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the law of truth, for equity, for righteousness’ sake, he did die: therefore is he a saint, therefore a martyr; but yet he died not for that Name whereby we are Christians, wherefore, save that the saying might be fulfilled, “I am alone, until I pass over.”
